effect: Delays activation of raw GameShark/Action Replay style cheat codes until after game startup.
notes: Recommended when using raw cheat codes; not required for special POPStarter commands such as $SMOOTH or $NOPAL.
Runtime hotkeys: Select+L1+R2 enables; Select+L2+R1 disables. Equivalent raw code observed: $S0003390 00000001.

effect: Disables automatic PAL patch; used to force PAL games into NTSC/240p behavior.
notes: Often requires $YPOS_## such as $YPOS_10 to recenter PAL games after NTSC conversion.

effect: Loads a null LibCrypt value into COP0 register.
notes: Mentioned for games with broken LibCrypt behavior, e.g. some Jackie Chan discs.

effect: Enables compatibility modes as an alternative to PATCH_X.BIN files.
notes: Example: $COMPATIBILITY_0x01 activates Mode 1.

effect: Sets startup delay for USB device initialization.
notes: Increase by 2–3 seconds for devices that work elsewhere but fail in POPStarter.

effect: Forces PAL patch and BIOS region behavior for VCDs missing valid PAL license text in boot sector.
notes: POPS runs in PAL mode and BIOS region code is patched to Europe.
Default observed: 640. Increase moves image left; decrease moves image right.
Common PAL-to-NTSC centering example: $YPOS_10.
Default observed: 2559.
Max observed: 2560. Decrease to adjust image right.

effect: Activates SetGsCrt hack for modern HDTV/component-display issues.
notes: Uses 480i for NTSC or 576i for PAL; helps displays that do not handle 240p/288p. Avoid as a default on CRT/composite because it can cause interlace flicker/problems.
Low compatibility. Not compatible with $XPOS, $YPOS, $DWSTRETCH, or $DWCROP.
Needs testing/visual examples.

effect: Recompiler/code-cache related option for slowdowns or random hangs.
notes: Do not use globally; can make many games fail.

effect: Sets POPS buffer to 1 sector instead of 16.
notes: May help some videos that hang.

effect: Probably related to game hangs and CDROM timeouts; variant of compatibility Mode 5.
notes: Needs primary old-wiki confirmation.
Can diagnose crashes caused by old game SFX/audio behavior.

effect: Enables GTE widescreen hack; changes apparent aspect/FOV.
notes: Does not fix HUDs, fonts, menus, 2D backgrounds, or render correction. Not compatible with every game.
Non-standard aspect. Same 2D/HUD caveats as $WIDESCREEN.
Same 2D/HUD caveats as $WIDESCREEN.
Applied automatically for physical PS1 CDROM mode according to ElOtroLado.
Counteracts $MUTE_CDDA behavior.
Confirm interaction with physical cards and per-game VMC naming.
Confirm interaction with physical cards and per-game VMC naming.

effect: Disables built-in game fixes integrated in POPStarter.
notes: May not fully restore original broken behavior for games whose issues were fixed by core POPS patches or later changes.

effect: Forces left analog stick to digital D-pad behavior for games without analog support.
notes: Leaves controller in digital behavior according to Spanish source.

effect: Alternative to $D2LS for games where $D2LS does not work.
notes: Leaves controller in analog behavior according to Spanish source.
Opens menu.
Opens menu.
Opens menu.

effect: L1+L2+R1+R2+X+Down closes POPS without IGR menu.
notes: Direct close/no menu.

effect: Start+Select closes POPS without IGR menu.
notes: Direct close/no menu.

effect: L1+L2+R1+R2+Start+Select closes POPS without IGR menu.
notes: krHACKen described this as OPL-like IGR with no popup. Last beta likely fixed this command.
Do not confuse with the separate 'disable ELF loader' workaround used to prevent POPStarter exit from chaining into BOOT.ELF.
